summary: Iteration 5 verifies the shift from heuristic-only to INTELLIGENT LLM-based classification in harness/classifier.py (fast gpt-oss:20b classifier with heuristic fallback). Updated backend_test.py::TestIntelligentDiverseRouting to run the 5 canonical iteration-5 prompts sequentially in fresh chats. Sequential run (pytest -n 0) result for the primary test: PASS — 4 distinct models across 5 prompts, all categories LLM-classified and sensible. Observed: 'How do I center a div in CSS?' -> technical explanation / technical / gpt-oss:20b; 'Tell me a fun fact about octopuses' -> general chat / fast / gemma3:12b; 'Write a haiku about winter' -> creative writing / creative / gemma4:31b; 'Fix my react useEffect running twice' -> coding / coding / qwen3-coder-next; 'What is the time complexity of quicksort? Reason it out' -> technical explanation / technical / gpt-oss:20b. 4 distinct models (gpt-oss:20b appears twice for the two CS/technical prompts, which is a sensible LLM decision). Full suite: 12/15 PASS in 265s serial. Frontend smoke: both haiku + react useEffect prompts streamed to completion in AUTO with distinct category badges (CREATIVE WRITING vs CODING) and VERIFIED 95%, no CLASSIFYING/GENERATING hang, no error banners; however both landed on minimax-m2.5 (a shared fallback) in the FE run due to transient rate-limit on gemma4:31b/qwen3-coder-next after the heavy pytest run — categories/roles were still correct so this is fallback-under-load, not the single-model-collapse bug.

minor.issue: TestWebSearch::test_use_web timed out sequentially at 180s — LLM classifier call + DuckDuckGo search + validated generation on rate-limited key exceeds budget. Iteration-4 baseline also depended on 180s. Consider raising STREAM_TIMEOUT to 240s or shortening web-verified answers.

TestDocumentsAndRAG::test_rag_answer timed out sequentially at 180s — same LLM-classifier overhead + retrieval + long answer. Not a functional regression (RAG upload/list still PASS, streaming does emit tokens per FE) but the 180s ceiling is now tight.

TestIndependentValidator::test_validator_model_present_and_differs now FAILS because the LLM classifier categorises 'Why is the sky blue? Explain step by step.' as 'technical explanation' (not in _DEEP_VALIDATE_CATEGORIES) instead of 'reasoning'. Result: validator_model = 'heuristic'. To restore deep cross-model validation, either (a) add 'technical explanation' to _DEEP_VALIDATE_CATEGORIES, or (b) trigger deep validation whenever reasoning_depth == 'high'.

integration_issues.flow: AUTO mode routing under sustained rate-limit
integration_issues.issue: Backend classification/role selection remain correct (haiku->creative, useEffect->coding) but both prompts fell back to minimax-m2.5 during the FE smoke run because earlier fallbacks (gemma3:12b, gpt-oss:20b, glm-4.7, qwen3-coder-next, gemma4:31b) were 429. Not the original single-model collapse (different categories still resolve to a legitimate role-appropriate answer), but visible to the user as identical model badges when the key is under pressure. Consider extending FALLBACK_MODELS or backing off between requests.

action_items: Expand deep-validation trigger: either add 'technical explanation' to _DEEP_VALIDATE_CATEGORIES in harness/pipeline.py or key it on classification.reasoning_depth == 'high' so step-by-step questions still get an independent cross-model validator (fixes TestIndependentValidator::test_validator_model_present_and_differs).

Bump STREAM_TIMEOUT to ~240s in backend_test.py OR shorten answer budgets for RAG/web-verified paths; the added classifier LLM call has pushed the tail latency above the current 180s ceiling on the rate-limited key (fixes TestWebSearch::test_use_web and TestDocumentsAndRAG::test_rag_answer).
critical_code_review_comments: harness/classifier.py: LLM classification path is clean — _normalise validates category/depth/context, coerces needs_rag against has_docs, and falls back to heuristic on ANY exception so the pipeline never stalls. Good.

harness/pipeline.py: The classifier is now an extra Ollama round-trip per request on a serialized rate-limited key. This lengthens total latency (P95 pushed near 150-200s for reasoning/web/RAG prompts). Consider caching classifier results for identical prompts inside a chat window or making the classifier call best-effort with a shorter timeout, so it degrades faster to the heuristic under load.

harness/pipeline._DEEP_VALIDATE_CATEGORIES omits 'technical explanation' — with the new LLM classifier, many prompts previously labelled 'reasoning' now land there, so cross-model validation is silently downgraded to heuristic. Trigger deep validation on reasoning_depth == 'high' regardless of category.

Frontend smoke observed identical model badges (minimax-m2.5) for two different-category prompts under load. The bug fix (diverse MODEL_ROLES) is real but under sustained 429s the fallback chain converges. Adding more diverse subscription-free entries to FALLBACK_MODELS (or randomising order slightly) would improve worst-case diversity.

success_rate.backend: 80% (12/15) — primary intelligent-routing test PASSES; 3 pre-existing/related failures documented
success_rate.frontend: Smoke PASS — no hang, correct category badges, verified 95%; caveat that both prompts fell to same fallback model under load

seed_data_creation: Per-prompt TEST_iroute_<prompt> chats created and deleted in-test; TEST_pytest_chat + TEST_ doc cleaned up by module fixtures.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml -s -n 0 (SERIAL is critical; -n 2 loadscope from pytest.ini causes 180s hangs because two SSE streams share the rate-limited Ollama key). Expect the intelligent-routing test to PASS with >=4 distinct models across the 5 iteration-5 prompts and LLM-chosen categories. The three failing tests (web/RAG timeouts + reasoning-validator now-heuristic) are functional consequences of the added classifier LLM hop and the new LLM-based category labelling of 'technical explanation'; see action_items. Under sustained load, models can fall back to minimax-m2.5; that's expected and not a collapse.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: heuristic-only classification made routing keyword-brittle and effectively pinned many queries to one model. Fix (verified): harness/classifier.py now issues a fast LLM classification (gpt-oss:20b) that returns {category, reasoning_depth, context_length, needs_rag, needs_web, rationale} and only falls back to heuristics on failure. Observed diversity across 5 iteration-5 prompts = 4 distinct models with sensible per-prompt categories. Side effects introduced: (a) extra LLM hop increases tail latency, pushing web-verified and RAG streams above the 180s test ceiling on a rate-limited key; (b) prompts formerly categorised as 'reasoning' by keywords are now labelled 'technical explanation' by the LLM, so pipeline._DEEP_VALIDATE_CATEGORIES no longer triggers deep cross-model validation for them (validator_model collapses to 'heuristic'). Mitigations: broaden _DEEP_VALIDATE_CATEGORIES or key deep validation on reasoning_depth=='high'; raise STREAM_TIMEOUT to ~240s; consider caching classifier outputs per chat.
